items.title: Grace Tame demands law reform after Diocese of Ballarat cleared of vicarious liability
items.summary: In every Australian state and the NT, churches cannot be held vicariously liable for historical child sexual abuse perpetrated by their priests. Grace Tame is leading the charge for change.

items.summary: The child safety regulator sought a law change after receiving "concerning" correspondence from Tasmania Police, which said its officers were not bound by a key child protection measure.

items.title: National park expansion fuels land price surge and anxiety of NSW farmers

items.title: Myanmar's women caught between war, disaster and blocked aid
items.summary: Women who had already lost their homes to last year's earthquake are now also cut off from essentials like sanitary pads and aid.

items.title: PM urged to intervene in youth justice systems to break the cycle of crime
items.summary: Hundreds of legal, health and children's organisations are calling on Anthony Albanese to convene an emergency summit to address the over-incarceration of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children.

items.summary: Stricter registration rules would weed out low-quality NDIS providers while delivering better services and budget savings, major operators say.

items.title: Boards make decisions affecting the nation but they don't look like Australia

items.summary: As fashion brands move away from animal leather, "vegan" has come to suggest something that is both kinder to animals and better for the planet. However, the reality is more complicated.

items.summary: A former Bondi nanny accused of torture and kidnapping for the Chilean Pinochet regime loses her final legal bid to avoid extradition to Chile.

items.title: Inside Delhi's growing black market for cooking gas after Strait of Hormuz closure

items.summary: Twelve new offences are set to be added to the government's adult crime, adult time legislation, including offences that no children have faced court on in the past 11 years.

items.title: Queensland maritime authorities criticised for 'slow' wrecked boats salvage
items.summary: The vessel 7 Wonder has sat wrecked at Rose Bay in north Queensland for more than two months. Locals say they have been picking up rubbish from the boat daily, but maritime authorities say local salvage contractors are "stretched".
